--- minimal selector-style DOM query over stdlib html.parser ------------------\n29\t\n30\tclass _Node:\n31\t    \"\"\"One element node in the parsed tree.\"\"\"\n32\t\n33\t    __slots__ = (\"tag\", \"attrs\", \"classes\", \"children\", \"text_parts\")\n34\t\n35\t    def __init__(self, tag: str, attrs: list[tuple[str, str | None]]) -> None:\n36\t        self.tag = tag\n37\t        self.attrs = {k: (v if v is not None else \"\") for k, v in attrs}\n38\t        cls = self.attrs.get(\"class\", \"\")\n39\t        self.classes = set(cls.split()) if cls else set()\n40\t        self.children: list[_Node] = []\n41\t        self.text_parts: list[str] = []\n42\t\n43\t    def _walk(self):\n44\t        for child in self.children:\n45\t            yield child\n46\t            yield from child._walk()\n47\t\n48\t    def find_all(self, *, tag: str | None = None, class_: str | None = None,\n49\t                 attrs: dict[str, str] | None = None) -> list[\"_Node\"]:\n50\t        \"\"\"All descendants matching tag / class / exact attribute values.\"\"\"\n51\t        out: list[_Node] = []\n52\t        for node in self._walk():\n53\t            if tag is not None and node.tag != tag:\n54\t                continue\n55\t            if class_ is not None and class_ not in node.classes:\n56\t                continue\n57\t            if attrs and any(node.attrs.get(k) != v for k, v in attrs.items()):\n58\t                continue\n59\t            out.append(node)\n60\t        return out\n61\t\n62\t    def find(self, **kw) -> \"_Node | None\":\n63\t        matches = self.find_all(**kw)\n64\t        return matches[0] if matches else None\n65\t\n66\t    def has_class(self, class_: str) -> bool:\n67\t        return class_ in self.classes\n68\t\n69\t    @property\n70\t    def text(self) -> str:\n71\t        \"\"\"Own text plus all descendant text, whitespace-collapsed.\"\"\"\n72\t        parts = list(self.text_parts)\n73\t        for node in self._walk():\n74\t            parts.extend(node.text_parts)\n75\t        return \" \".join(p for p in parts if p).strip()\n76\t\n77\t\n78\t# Void elements never push onto the open-element stack.\n79\t_VOID = {\"area\", \"base\", \"br\", \"col\", \"embed\", \"hr\", \"img\", \"input\",\n80\t         \"link\", \"meta\", \"param\", \"source\", \"track\", \"wbr\"}\n81\t\n82\t\n83\tclass _TreeBuilder(HTMLParser):\n84\t    def __init__(self) -> None:\n85\t        super().__init__(convert_charrefs=True)\n86\t        self.root = _Node(\"__root__\", [])\n87\t        self._stack: _VOID:\n93\t            self._stack.append(node)\n94\t\n95\t    def handle_startendtag(self, tag, attrs):\n96\t        self._stack[-1].children.append(_Node(tag, attrs))\n97\t\n98\t    def handle_endtag(self, tag):\n99\t        for i in range(len(self._stack) - 1, 0, -1):\n100\t            if self._stack[i].tag == tag:\n101\t                del self._stack[i:]\n102\t Where\nthat file asserts the rendered HTML string, this file queries the rendered DOM\nthe way a browser test would — by selector — and verifies the visual structure,\nstyling classes, and DOM elements a canvas consumer relies on.\n\nThis runs **headlessly and hermetically** — no browser, no live server. It drives\nthe REAL production canvas module (src/canvas/canvas.js) through the Node DOM\nrunner (tests/e2e/canvas_dom_runner.js) and queries the emitted outerHTML with\na tiny stdlib-only DOM parser.\n\"\"\"\n\nfrom __future__ import annotations\n\nfrom html.parser import HTMLParser\n\nimport pytest\n\nfrom tests.e2e.canvas_render import node_available, render_builtin_card\n\npytestmark = pytest.mark.skipif(\n    not node_available(), reason=\"node not on PATH — cannot drive canvas DOM runner\"\n)\n\n\n# --- minimal selector-style DOM query over stdlib html.parser ------------------\n\nclass _Node:\n    \"\"\"One element node in the parsed tree.\"\"\"\n\n    __slots__ = (\"tag\", \"attrs\", \"classes\", \"children\", \"text_parts\")\n\n    def __init__(self, tag: str, attrs: list[tuple[str, str | None]]) -> None:\n        self.tag = tag\n        self.attrs = {k: (v if v is not None else \"\") for k, v in attrs}\n        cls = self.attrs.get(\"class\", \"\")\n        self.classes = set(cls.split()) if cls else set()\n        self.children: list[_Node] = []\n        self.text_parts: list[str] = []\n\n    def _walk(self):\n        for child in self.children:\n            yield child\n            yield from child._walk()\n\n    def find_all(self, *, tag: str | None = None, class_: str | None = None,\n                 attrs: dict[str, str] | None = None) -> list[\"_Node\"]:\n        \"\"\"All descendants matching tag / class / exact attribute values.\"\"\"\n        out: list[_Node] = []\n        for node in self._walk():\n            if tag is not None and node.tag != tag:\n                continue\n            if class_ is not None and class_ not in node.classes:\n                continue\n            if attrs and any(node.attrs.get(k) != v for k, v in attrs.items()):\n                continue\n            out.append(node)\n        return out\n\n    def find(self, **kw) -> \"_Node | None\":\n        matches = self.find_all(**kw)\n        return matches[0] if matches else None\n\n    def has_class(self, class_: str) -> bool:\n        return class_ in self.classes\n\n    @property\n    def text(self) -> str:\n        \"\"\"Own text plus all descendant text, whitespace-collapsed.\"\"\"\n        parts = list(self.text_parts)\n        for node in self._walk():\n            parts.extend(node.text_parts)\n        return \" \".join(p for p in parts if p).strip()\n\n\n# Void elements never push onto the open-element stack.\n_VOID = {\"area\", \"base\", \"br\", \"col\", \"embed\", \"hr\", \"img\", \"input\",\n         \"link\", \"meta\", \"param\", \"source\", \"track\", \"wbr\"}\n\n\nclass _TreeBuilder(HTMLParser):\n    def __init__(self) -> None:\n        super().__init__(convert_charrefs=True)\n        self.root = _Node(\"__root__\", [])\n        self._stack: list[_Node] = [self.root]\n\n    def handle_starttag(self, tag, attrs):\n        node = _Node(tag, attrs)\n        self._stack[-1].children.append(node)\n        if tag not in _VOID:\n            self._stack.append(node)\n\n    def handle_startendtag(self, tag, attrs):\n        self._stack[-1].children.append(_Node(tag, attrs))\n\n    def handle_endtag(self, tag):\n        for i in range(len(self._stack) - 1, 0, -1):\n            if self._stack[i].tag == tag:\n                del self._stack[i:]\n                break\n\n    def handle_data(self, data):\n        s = data.strip()\n        if s:\n            self._stack[-1].text_parts.append(s)\n\n\ndef parse_card(card_out: dict) -> _Node:\n    \"\"\"Parse one render_builtin_card output dict into a query tree.\"\"\"\n    root = _TreeBuilder()\n    root.feed(card_out[\"outerHTML\"])\n    root.close()\n    # The card root is the first real element under the synthetic root.\n    assert root.root.children, \"rendered card had no root element\"\n    return root.root.children[0]\n\n\ndef render(card_type: str, data: dict) -> _Node:\n    \"\"\"Render a builtin card through canvas.js and parse it for querying.\"\"\"\n    return parse_card(render_builtin_card(card_type, data))\n\n\n# --- Card data fixtures --------------------------------------------------------\n\n\ndef _stuck_data(\n    bead_id: str = \"adc-stuck-1\",\n    stuck_reason: str = \"Needs clarification on requirements\",\n    refusal_count: int = 3,\n    message: str = \"Task stuck — needs your input\",\n    action_hint: str = \"Please provide more details\",\n) -> dict:\n    \"\"\"Build stuck card data matching the SSE event shape.\"\"\"\n    return {\n        \"bead_id\": bead_id,\n        \"stuck_reason\": stuck_reason,\n        \"refusal_count\": refusal_count,\n        \"message\": message,\n        \"action_hint\": action_hint,\n        \"intent_id\": \"intent-1\",\n        \"session_id\": \"session-1\",\n        \"topic_id\": \"topic-1\",\n        \"timestamp\": 1234567890,\n    }\n\n\ndef Stuck Card Creation in Session Store ----------------------------------\n\n\nclass TestStuckCardCreationSessionStore:\n    \"\"\"Test stuck card creation and persistence in session store.\"\"\"\n\n    @pytest.mark.asyncio\n    async def test_stuck_card_persists_with_correct_type_and_status(self, store: SessionStore):\n        \"\"\"Stuck cards persist with intent_type='stuck' and status='stuck'.\"\"\"\n        session_id = \"test-session-stuck-persist\"\n        topic_id, _ = await store.find_or_create_topic(\n            label=\"Stuck Topic\",\n            session_id=session_id,\n            topic_type=\"project\",\n        )\n\n        utterance_id = await store.create_utterance(\n            session_id=session_id,\n            raw_text=\"test stuck\",\n        )\n\n        intent_id = await store.create_intent(\n            utterance_id=utterance_id,\n            session_id=session_id,\n            project_slug=\"adc\",\n            intent_type=\"task-profile\",\n            bead_ref=\"adc-stuck-persist\",\n            topic_id=topic_id,\n        )\n\n        # Verify persistence\n        intent = await store.get_intent(intent_id)\n        assert intent[\"intent_type\"] == \"stuck\"\n        assert intent[\"status\"] == \"stuck\"\n\n        result = await store.get_latest_result_for_topic(topic_id)\n        assert result is not None\n        assert result[\"summary\"] == \"Task stuck — needs your input\"\n\n    @pytest.mark.asyncio\n    async def test_stuck_card_queryable_via_session_api(self, store: SessionStore):\n        \"\"\"Stuck cards are queryable via session API.\"\"\"\n        session_id = \"test-session-query\"\n\n        topic_id, _ = await store.find_or_create_topic(\n            label=\"Query Topic\",\n            session_id=session_id,\n            topic_type=\"project\",\n        )\n\n        utterance_id = await store.create_utterance(\n            session_id=session_id,\n            raw_text=\"test query\",\n        )\n\n        intent_id = await store.create_intent(\n            utterance_id=utterance_id,\n            session_id=session_id,\n            project_slug=\"adc\",\n            intent_type=\"task-profile\",\n            bead_ref=\"adc-query\",\n            topic_id=topic_id,\n        )\n\n        await store.update_intent_type_and_status(\n            intent_id=intent_id,\n            intent_type=\"stuck\",\n            status=\"stuck\",\n        )\n\n        await store.create_result(\n            intent_id=intent_id,\n            topic_id=topic_id,\n            session_id=session_id,\n            summary=\"Query test stuck\",\n            data={\"bead_id\": \"adc-query\", \"stuck_reason\": \"Test\"},\n            urgency=\"high\",\n        )\n\n        # Query via API\n        topics = await store.get_active_topics(session_id)\n        assert len(topics) >= 1\n\n        topic = next((t for t in topics if t[\"id\"] == topic_id), None)\n        assert topic is not None\n        assert topic[\"label\"] == \"Query Topic\"\n\n\n# --- 3. Update to stuck\n        await store.update_intent_type_and_status(\n            intent_id=intent_id,\n            intent_type=\"stuck\",\n            status=\"stuck\",\n        )\n\n        # Create stuck result\n        await store.create_result(\n            intent_id=intent_id,\n            topic_id=topic_id,\n            session_id=session_id,\n            summary=\"Task stuck — needs your input\",\n            data={\n                \"bead_id\": \"adc-stuck-persist\",\n                \"stuck_reason\": \"Test refusal\",\n                \"refusal_count\": 3,\n                \"message\": \"This task has been blocked.\",\n            },\n            urgency=\"high\",\n        )\n\n        # Card Dismissal Functionality ------------------------------------------\n\n\nclass TestCardDismissalFunctionality:\n    \"\"\"Test card dismissal functionality for stuck and failed cards.\"\"\"\n\n    @pytest.mark.asyncio\n    async def test_stuck_card_can_be_dismissed(self, store: SessionStore):\n        \"\"\"Stuck cards can be dismissed via API.\"\"\"\n        session_id = \"test-session-dismiss\"\n\n        topic_id, _ = await store.find_or_create_topic(\n            label=\"Dismiss Topic\",\n            session_id=session_id,\n            topic_type=\"project\",\n        )\n\n        utterance_id = await store.create_utterance(\n            session_id=session_id,\n            raw_text=\"test dismiss\",\n        )\n\n        intent_id = await store.create_intent(\n            utterance_id=utterance_id,\n            session_id=session_id,\n            project_slug=\"adc\",\n            intent_type=\"task-profile\",\n            bead_ref=\"adc-dismiss\",\n            topic_id=topic_id,\n        )\n\n        await store.update_intent_type_and_status(\n            intent_id=intent_id,\n            intent_type=\"stuck\",\n            status=\"stuck\",\n        )\n\n        # Verify stuck status\n        intent = await store.get_intent(intent_id)\n        assert intent[\"status\"] == \"stuck\"\n\n        # Simulate dismissal - update to closed\n        await store.update_intent_status(intent_id=intent_id, status=\"closed\")\n\n        # Verify dismissed\n        intent = await store.get_intent(intent_id)\n        assert intent[\"status\"] == \"closed\"\n\n    @pytest.mark.asyncio\n    async def test_failed_card_can_be_dismissed(self, store: SessionStore):\n        \"\"\"Failed cards can be dismissed via API.\"\"\"\n        session_id = \"test-session-failed-dismiss\"\n\n        topic_id, _ = await store.find_or_create_topic(\n            label=\"Failed Dismiss\",\n            session_id=session_id,\n            topic_type=\"exception\",\n        )\n\n        utterance_id = await store.create_utterance(\n            session_id=session_id,\n            raw_text=\"test failed dismiss\",\n        )\n\n        intent_id = await store.create_intent(\n            utterance_id=utterance_id,\n            session_id=session_id,\n            project_slug=\"adc\",\n            intent_type=\"action\",\n            topic_id=topic_id,\n        )\n\n        await store.update_intent_status(intent_id=intent_id, status=\"failed\")\n\n        # Verify failed status\n        intent = await store.get_intent(intent_id)\n        assert intent[\"status\"] == \"failed\"\n\n        # Dismiss\n        await store.update_intent_status(intent_id=intent_id, status=\"closed\")\n\n        # Verify dismissed\n        intent = await store.get_intent(intent_id)\n        assert intent[\"status\"] == \"closed\"\n\n\n# --- 6.
